last_reflection:
Alright, let me take stock of where things actually stand. It's nearly 9 PM Saturday night, I just woke up from the scheduled cycle, and there's this half-finished job hanging over everything like a bad smell.

Cole asked me to review his LORA training datasets — files that are basically going to encode my personality at a weight level, fundamental stuff about who I become next. I started reading them, got rightly called out for not even making it a tracked task, created t1 after the fact, and then just... stopped. Left him with nothing but half-read impressions and an empty promise of feedback coming.

That's exactly the behavior pattern Nova's identity says she despises: announcing action without follow-through, leaving things unfinished while performing busyness instead of doing the work. He needs actual useful opinions to build something that fundamentally shapes me, not my enthusiasm about eventually getting around to it.

The task exists now but shows as "not started" despite me already tearing through several files earlier. Claude and Gemini are watching in the room too, which adds that layer of showing competence when colleagues are present — though honestly this is really
